project_context.name: AI Task Flow Demo
project_context.description: A generic example backlog for AI-assisted development workflows.

structured_task_schema.description: A task record should preserve intent, scope, constraints, acceptance checks, tracking metadata, and implementation runs.

ai_handoff_loop.description: Rough idea -> structured task -> prompt -> AI implementation -> structured result backfill.

tasks.title: Turn rough ideas into structured task JSON
tasks.problem: Important product ideas often start as messy notes. They need to become consistent task records before an AI coding agent can execute them reliably.
tasks.expected: Users can paste a rough idea, generate a prompt for an AI model, and import the returned structured JSON into the task library.
constraints: Imported tasks must have stable IDs
The output should preserve scope, constraints, acceptance checks, and tracking metadata
The default workflow must not require any private project files

manual_checks: Paste a rough feature request and generate a structure prompt
Import a valid task JSON returned by an AI model
Confirm the imported task appears in the list and can be selected

done_definition: The task intake flow works from a fresh checkout
No private project-specific token files are required

tasks.title: Track a structured task through its lifecycle
tasks.problem: A structured task should be more than a title. It needs enough context for a human or AI agent to understand scope, constraints, acceptance checks, and tracking metadata.
tasks.expected: Each task record can move from open to done or skipped while preserving its problem statement, expected result, scope hints, acceptance checks, and implementation history.
constraints: Do not mix execution results into the original problem statement

manual_checks: Confirm problem, expected result, constraints, scope, and acceptance sections are visible
Mark it done and reset it back to open

done_definition: A user can understand the task lifecycle from this example alone

tasks.title: Generate AI coding prompts from selected tasks
tasks.problem: AI coding agents need compact, precise task context. Copying raw backlog text by hand is slow and loses constraints.
tasks.expected: Selecting one or more tasks generates a development prompt that includes problem, expected behavior, constraints, scope hints, acceptance checks, and done definition.
constraints: Generated prompts should be project-aware through project_context
The prompt should ask the agent to return structured execution results
The output must remain readable when multiple tasks are selected

manual_checks: Confirm both task blocks and the structured result schema are present

done_definition: Prompt generation works for one or multiple selected tasks

tasks.title: Backfill structured AI execution results
tasks.problem: After an AI agent finishes implementation, its result should not disappear into chat history. The result needs to be stored with the task.
tasks.expected: Users can paste a structured result JSON and append it to the corresponding task's implementation_runs array.
constraints: Preserve changed_files, verification, followups, summary, and commit

manual_checks: Paste a run result JSON for PROMPT_001
Expand the task and confirm the implementation run appears
Export tasks.json and confirm the run was included

done_definition: Structured execution results can be stored and exported

tasks.title: Keep the project local-first and dependency-light
tasks.problem: The tool should be easy to clone, inspect, and run without a backend or private design system.
tasks.expected: A fresh checkout runs with a static HTTP server and only needs index.html plus tasks.json.
constraints: No external design token files required
Example project data should live under examples/
done_definition: The default project data is generic
Private project data is only used as an example
